key_insight: Ultrafast high-temperature sintering enables zirconia-copper cermets to retain up to 50 wt% metastable zirconia phase while preserving a fine, homogeneous microstructure.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical route to tougher, more functional copper-based composites for transformation toughening, self-healing, and crack-detection applications.
method: Joule-heated samples rapidly to about 900 °C at roughly 100 °C/s.
Used only a 20 s hold to limit grain growth and matrix relaxation.
Kinetically favored formation of metastable high-temperature zirconia in the copper matrix.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful retention of up to 50 wt% metastable phase at room temperature together with suppressed copper coarsening and refined microstructure.
watch_item: The approach depends on tightly controlled ultrafast thermal processing, so scale-up and thermal uniformity may be challenging for larger or more complex parts.
paper_title: Leveraging rapid sintering to retain metastable zirconia in copper
